title: Map a Bill Through Parliament
description: Take one Australian bill and trace its movement through drafting, debate, scrutiny, amendment risk, assent, and implementation.
whyItMatters: You will stop confusing political announcements with actual legal change once you can track a bill as a funnel instead of a speech.

steps.title: Select a bill
steps.description: Choose a government bill or significant private member's bill with enough public material to trace.

steps.title: Build the funnel
steps.description: Map drafting, introduction, debates, scrutiny, possible amendment points, assent, and post-passage needs.

steps.title: Find the leverage point
steps.description: Identify where the bill is most vulnerable to negotiation, delay, or dilution.

deliverables: A one-page note on the decisive stage and why it mattered

rubric.description: Does the work show the difference between debate, scrutiny, assent, and implementation?
Does it identify where the real leverage or blockage sits?
